title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence · Behavior Case: Parser Selection
summary: A selector works under one parser but fails under another because omitted elements, foster parenting, broken nesting, and script or noscript state produce different trees. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parser selection increment in bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.
Predict omitted tags, mismatched nesting, duplicate attributes, character references, templates, foreign content, and recovery effects on target nodes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-parser-selection — Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_html_parser_models_5(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build Parser Selection evidence · Theory Parser Selection

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-parser-selection-1v0hcp
# Competency: scraper-parser-selection.
# Case 5741: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_parser_selection_1v0hcp(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1v0hcp = "1v0hcp-pch0v1-5741"
    assert evidence_variant_1v0hcp
    evidence_axes_1v0hcp = (
        "authorization1v0hcp",
        "url5741",
        "responsepch0v1",
        "tree1v0hcp5741",
        "frontier57411v0hcp",
        "reportpch0v15741",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v0hcp)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Parser Selection structural check is only one gate. bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Parser Selection evidence · Theory Parser Selection, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Parser Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Parser Selection evidence · Theory Parser Selection, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 5986 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Selection model · Theory Parser Selection
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Parser Selection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 7605 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Selection model · Theory Parser Selection.
steps.why: Retrieving Parser Selection through the concrete bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Parser Selection model · Theory Parser Selection, keep Parser Selection connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: scraper-parser-selection-1v0hcp.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-parser-selection-1v0hcp(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_parser_selection_1v0hcp\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?tokenized != fixtures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?executed_scripts > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
